- If new public symbols are exposed, they are added to `almaapitk/__init__.py` `__all__` and the lazy import map.

## Notes for the implementing agent
- Mirror an existing domain method as the pattern source (e.g., `Acquisitions.get_invoice` for read, `Acquisitions.create_invoice_simple` for write).
- All public methods must have type hints + Google-style docstrings.
- Validate required inputs at top of method and raise `AlmaValidationError` with a clear message.
- Log method entry with key parameters (info), success with result identifiers (info), and errors with full context (error).
- NEVER print — use `self.logger`.
- Test in SANDBOX first. Add at least one unit test under `tests/unit/` and one integration test under `tests/integration/`.
- After implementation, `poetry run python scripts/smoke_import.py` and the public-API contract test MUST pass.

- LOW PRIORITY. Single-method addition; useful for discovering report directory structure programmatically.
- IMPORTANT: per project memory, Analytics requires PRODUCTION credentials (single shared DB; no SANDBOX endpoint). Document this in the new methods' docstrings.

body:
**Complexity:** L
**Benefit:** High (matches the actual shape of consumer projects)

## Problem

Per CLAUDE.md, all four extracted consumer projects share the same shape:

| Project | Input | Output |
|---|---|---|
| `Alma-update-expired-users-emails` | CSV/Excel of users | annotated CSV |
| `Alma-RS-lending-request-automation` | borrowing requests | annotated CSV + log |
| `Alma-Digital-Upload` | local files + manifest | upload report |
| `Alma-Acquisitions-Automation` | Rialto export | invoice report |

Each project re-implements the same scaffolding: read CSV / DataFrame, normalize column names, iterate rows, call Alma, capture per-row success/failure, write annotated output, support dry-run, handle partial progress on crash. Hundreds of lines duplicated across repos.

## Proposal

A `BatchRunner` class in the toolkit, building on the async bulk-call primitive (separate issue):

```python
from almaapitk import BatchRunner

runner = BatchRunner(
    client=alma,
    operation=lambda client, row: client.users.get_user(row["primary_id"]),
    rows=df,                                  # pandas DataFrame or list[dict]
    output_dir="output/run-2025-04-30/",
    batch_size=500,
    throttle_rps=25,
    on_error="collect",                       # "collect" | "raise" | "skip"
    checkpoint_every=500,
    dry_run=False,                             # CLAUDE.md mandates dry-run-default
    progress=True,                             # tqdm bar
)
result = runner.run()
# result.successes, result.errors, result.checkpoint_path
```

Behaviors:

- Reads pandas DataFrame OR list[dict]; column-name normalization via `convert_column_case` style helper.
- Each row → one async invocation (via the bulk-call primitive in #18).
- Per-row JSON output saved under `output_dir/responses/{idx}.json` (configurable).
- After every `checkpoint_every` rows: write `checkpoint.json` (last completed idx + summary). On relaunch with same `output_dir`, resume from the checkpoint.
- Final outputs: annotated input CSV (success / error / tracking_id columns appended) and a run summary JSON.
- Dry-run mode: validate inputs, log the planned operations, do NOT call Alma.
- All operations logged through the existing `alma_logging` framework.

<!-- prereqs:auto:begin -->
## Prerequisites

**Hard blockers — must merge before this issue is ready to start:**
- #18 — Async / concurrent bulk-call primitive

**Recommended (would simplify or improve this work, but not strictly blocking):**
- #10 — Propagate tracking_id and alma_code on errors (error rows carry tracking IDs)
- #14 — Replace print() with logger; remove safe_request() (BatchRunner must not print)

<!-- prereqs:auto:end -->

## Acceptance criteria

- `BatchRunner` class in public API, lazy-imported.
- pandas as an optional dep: `pip install almaapitk[batch]`.
- Resume-from-checkpoint demonstrated in tests (kill mid-run, relaunch, verify it picks up).
- Each consumer project (per CLAUDE.md) can migrate to `BatchRunner` in ≤50 LOC; an example migration committed for one of them as proof.
- Documented in CLAUDE.md as the recommended path for new ops scripts.
- Dry-run default matches CLAUDE.md "Safety-First Design" guidance.


## Notes

This converts AlmaAPITK from "an HTTP client + domain methods" into "an HTTP client + domain methods + the runner that consumer projects actually need." Highest-leverage user-facing addition once the async foundation is in place.
title: API: CSV/DataFrame BatchRunner with progress + checkpointing

body:
**Complexity:** L
**Benefit:** Medium

## Problem

Ex Libris publishes OpenAPI specs for every Alma API surface (bibs, conf, electronic, users, acq, analytics) at https://developers.exlibrisgroup.com/. AlmaAPITK ignores them. Consequences:

- Request payload errors (missing required fields, wrong types) only surface after a network round-trip, producing opaque Alma error messages.
- There is no programmatic source of truth for which params are accepted by which endpoint.
- The Pydantic-models effort (#12) is hand-modeling what the specs already describe.

## Proposal

A new opt-in `almaapitk.openapi` module:

1. **Vendor the specs**: download the published OpenAPI JSON files into `src/almaapitk/openapi/specs/` with a version stamp. Include a `scripts/refresh_openapi_specs.py` to refresh on demand.
2. **Validate requests** (opt-in, off by default):
   ```python
   alma = AlmaAPIClient("SANDBOX", validate_requests=True)
   alma.get("almaws/v1/bibs/123", params={"view": "made-up-value"})
   # raises AlmaValidationError before the HTTP call:
   # "params.view='made-up-value' not in enum [brief, full]"
   ```
3. **Validate responses** (opt-in, off by default) — useful in tests / CI.
4. **Power Pydantic-model generation** (#12) from the spec rather than hand-writing.

Implementation sketch:
```python
class OpenAPIValidator:
    def __init__(self, specs_dir: Path):
        self._specs = self._load_all(specs_dir)   # dict[endpoint -> openapi op]

    def validate_request(self, method: str, endpoint: str,
                         params: dict | None, body: Any) -> None:
        op = self._lookup(method, endpoint)
        if op is None:
            return                                 # endpoint not in spec → don't block
        self._check_params(op, params)
        self._check_body(op, body)
```

Use `openapi-core` or `jsonschema` for the actual validation; both are pure-Python.

<!-- prereqs:auto:begin -->
## Prerequisites

_None — this issue has no upstream dependencies and does not have recommended soft prereqs at the toolkit level._

<!-- prereqs:auto:end -->

## Acceptance criteria

- Specs vendored under `src/almaapitk/openapi/specs/` with a `manifest.json` recording version + fetch date for each.
- `AlmaAPIClient(validate_requests=False)` is the default — zero behavior change for existing callers.
- `AlmaAPIClient(validate_requests=True)` raises `AlmaValidationError` with a clear field path on bad input.
- `scripts/refresh_openapi_specs.py` fetches the latest specs and writes them with updated manifest.
- `openapi-core` (or chosen validator) is an optional dep: `pip install almaapitk[openapi]`.
- Tests cover: spec lookup hit, spec lookup miss (passes through), validation failure, valid request.
- Documented in CLAUDE.md.

## Related

- #12 (Pydantic models) — once the specs are vendored, model generation can derive from them, reducing hand-modeling.
- #9 (error registry) — validation errors raised pre-flight should use the same exception hierarchy.

## Notes

Must default to OFF so the library works offline and so we don't surprise callers with newly-strict validation. The point is to give callers an opt-in safety net.
title: API: optional OpenAPI-driven request/response validation

body:
**Complexity:** M (1–3 days)
**Benefit:** Medium–High (caller ergonomics for bibs work)

## Problem

MARC handling in `src/almaapitk/domains/bibs.py:211-355` is hand-rolled XML manipulation:

- `update_marc_field` (L211)
- `_build_updated_marc_xml` (L266)
- `_add_datafield` (L319)
- `_sanitize_xml_text` (L332)
- `_extract_marc_subfield_values` (L17)

Each call re-parses the MARC XML, mutates one field, re-serializes. Common workflows (set multiple fields in one update, conditionally add a field, copy fields between records, deduplicate subfields) require either chaining several `update_marc_field` calls (multiple round-trips, no atomicity) or callers reaching into the raw XML themselves. The package re-implements work that `pymarc` does well.

Concrete pain in caller code today:

```python
bibs.update_marc_field(mms_id, "594", {"a": "value"}, ind1="0", ind2="0")
bibs.update_marc_field(mms_id, "246", {"a": "subtitle"})   # second round-trip
bibs.update_marc_field(mms_id, "650", {"a": "subject"})    # third round-trip
```

## Proposal

Recommended: integrate pymarc as an optional dependency.

```python
# new methods on BibliographicRecords
def get_record_marc(self, mms_id: str) -> pymarc.Record:
    """Fetch and parse the MARC record into a pymarc.Record object."""

def update_record_marc(self, mms_id: str, record: pymarc.Record,
                       validate: bool = True) -> AlmaResponse:
    """Serialize a pymarc.Record back to Alma."""
```

Caller code becomes:

```python
record = bibs.get_record_marc(mms_id)
record.add_ordered_field(
    pymarc.Field(tag="594", indicators=["0", "0"],
                 subfields=[pymarc.Subfield(code="a", value="value")])
)
record.add_ordered_field(pymarc.Field(tag="246", subfields=[...]))
record.add_ordered_field(pymarc.Field(tag="650", subfields=[...]))
bibs.update_record_marc(mms_id, record)   # one round-trip
```

Alternative: implement an in-house `MarcRecord`/`Field`/`Subfield` mini-API (lighter dep but reinventing pymarc).

<!-- prereqs:auto:begin -->
## Prerequisites

_None — this issue has no upstream dependencies and does not have recommended soft prereqs at the toolkit level._

<!-- prereqs:auto:end -->

## Acceptance criteria

- pymarc added as optional dependency: `pip install almaapitk[marc]`.
- New methods `get_record_marc` and `update_record_marc` on `BibliographicRecords`.
- Existing `update_marc_field` / `_build_updated_marc_xml` / `_add_datafield` / `_sanitize_xml_text` are preserved (no breaking change). Mark the new path as the recommended one in docstrings + CLAUDE.md.
- Round-trip test: fetch a sandbox record, mutate two fields locally, push back, re-fetch, assert.
- README and CLAUDE.md updated with an example of the new pattern.

## Notes

Once the pymarc-based path proves itself, the long-term plan can be to deprecate the bespoke XML helpers in a major version. Out of scope for this issue.
title: API: dedicated MARC manipulation layer (consider pymarc integration)

body:
**Complexity:** L (>3 days)
**Benefit:** High

## Problem

AlmaAPITK has zero concurrency primitives. Every workflow that needs to update many users, receive many POL items, fetch many invoices, or scan many records issues calls serially. With Alma's ~25 RPS per-institution cap, a perfectly paced 1,000-item bulk is ~40s wall-clock minimum; today's serial calls take minutes. Concrete cases in the consumer projects (per CLAUDE.md):

- `Alma-update-expired-users-emails` — touches every expired user, currently serial.
- `Alma-RS-lending-request-automation` — partner sweeps.
- `Alma-Acquisitions-Automation` — bulk POL receiving and invoice creation.

## Proposal

A new `almaapitk.aio` (or `almaapitk.async`) module with an async client and a generic bulk-call helper.

```python
# Async client mirroring the sync surface
class AsyncAlmaAPIClient:
    """aiohttp-backed async sibling of AlmaAPIClient.
    Honors the same env vars, headers, region/host config, retries, and rate-limit
    knobs as the sync client.
    """
    async def get(self, endpoint, *, params=None) -> AlmaResponse: ...
    async def post(self, endpoint, *, data=None, params=None) -> AlmaResponse: ...
    # etc.

# Bulk runner — sync entry point, async under the hood.
def bulk_call(
    client,                               # sync or async client
    items,                                # iterable of inputs
    operation,                            # callable: (client, item) -> result
    *,
    throttle_rps: int = 25,
    max_concurrency: int = 25,
    on_error: str = "collect",            # "collect" | "raise" | "skip"
    progress: bool = True,
) -> tuple[list, list]:
    """Returns (successes, errors). Each error is {idx, item, exception, tracking_id}."""
```

Implementation notes:
- Use `asyncio.Semaphore(max_concurrency)` to bound in-flight requests (independent from RPS cap).
- Use a token-bucket throttler (e.g., `asyncio_throttle.Throttler` or a small built-in) for RPS pacing.
- Wire 429 handling: respect `Retry-After`, decrement a per-item attempt counter, then collect/raise.
- Per-item errors carry `(idx, original_item, exception_or_response, tracking_id)` so the caller can write an annotated output.
- Sync entry point wraps `asyncio.run(...)` so existing sync scripts can adopt without converting.

Optional install:
```
pip install almaapitk[async]   # aiohttp + asyncio_throttle
```

<!-- prereqs:auto:begin -->
## Prerequisites

**Hard blockers — must merge before this issue is ready to start:**
- #3 — Persistent requests.Session
- #5 — Retry with exponential backoff for 429 / 5xx

**Recommended (would simplify or improve this work, but not strictly blocking):**
- #8 — Client-side rolling-window rate limiting (share RPS cap config across sync and async paths)

<!-- prereqs:auto:end -->

## Acceptance criteria

- New `AsyncAlmaAPIClient` covering at minimum GET/POST/PUT/DELETE.
- New `bulk_call(...)` (or method on the client) that accepts an iterable + a coroutine function.
- Per-item error capture: a single failure does not abort the batch.
- Throttler enforces an RPS cap from the constructor; default 25.
- 429 + Retry-After honored.
- Tests using `aioresponses` covering: happy path, mixed-success, 429-then-success, full-batch failure.
- aiohttp / asyncio_throttle are optional dependencies; library imports cleanly without them.
- Public API export added in `almaapitk.__init__` lazy-import map.
- CLAUDE.md gains a section on when to choose async vs. sync.


## Notes

This is the largest functional gap exposed by the comparison work. Pairs naturally with the batch-runner issue (separate) which builds on top of `bulk_call`.
title: API: add async/concurrent bulk-call primitive (asyncio + aiohttp)

       "Operating System :: OS Independent",

body:
**Complexity:** S
**Benefit:** Low

## Problems (cumulative correctness fixes)

1. **Bare `except:` clauses** at `src/almaapitk/client/AlmaAPIClient.py:147,187,251,311` mask `KeyboardInterrupt`, `SystemExit`, and any unrelated bugs (typos, import errors). The intent is "ignore JSON-decode failures."
2. **`AlmaResponse.data` re-parses JSON on every access** (`AlmaAPIClient.py:30-32`). A caller writing `if r.data and r.data.get("foo"):` parses twice; for a large analytics report, that is measurable.
3. **Repeated `try: response.json() except: None`** in `get/post/put` should be a single helper.

## Proposal

1. Narrow excepts to specific exceptions:
   ```python
   except (ValueError, KeyError, json.JSONDecodeError):
       ...
   ```
2. Cache the parsed body on `AlmaResponse`:
   ```python
   @property
   def data(self):
       if not hasattr(self, "_cached_body"):
           self._cached_body = self._response.json() if self._response.content else None
       return self._cached_body
   ```
3. Extract a helper:
   ```python
   def _safe_response_body(response):
       if "application/json" not in response.headers.get("content-type", ""):
           return None
       try:
           return response.json()
       except (ValueError, json.JSONDecodeError):
           return None
   ```

<!-- prereqs:auto:begin -->
## Prerequisites

**Recommended (would simplify or improve this work, but not strictly blocking):**
- #4 — Consolidate HTTP verbs into _request() (pairs naturally with verb consolidation)

<!-- prereqs:auto:end -->

## Acceptance criteria

- No bare `except:` in `src/almaapitk/client/`.
- `AlmaResponse._response.json()` is called at most once per response across `.json()`, `.data`, and the debug-body-logging path.
- `_safe_response_body` (or equivalent) is the single place that decides whether to call `response.json()`.
- A unit test asserts `.data` is parsed exactly once even when accessed multiple times.
title: Quality: tighten exception handling and cache AlmaResponse.data

body:
**Complexity:** M
**Benefit:** Low

## Problem

Today, callers have to instantiate domain classes explicitly:

```python
from almaapitk import AlmaAPIClient, Acquisitions, BibliographicRecords

client = AlmaAPIClient("SANDBOX")
acq = Acquisitions(client)
bibs = BibliographicRecords(client)
inv = acq.get_invoice("123")
```

A more idiomatic style mirrors Alma's URL tree and reads naturally:

```python
client = AlmaAPIClient("SANDBOX")
inv = client.acq.invoices.get_invoice("123")
bib = client.bibs.records.get_record(mms_id)
user = client.users.get_user(primary_id)
```

This is a pure ergonomic upgrade and can ship without breaking the existing `Acquisitions(client)` API.

## Proposal

Lazy properties on `AlmaAPIClient` that construct domain façades on first access and cache them:

```python
@property
def acq(self):
    if not hasattr(self, "_acq"):
        from almaapitk.domains.acquisition import Acquisitions
        self._acq = Acquisitions(self)
    return self._acq
```

Repeat for `.bibs`, `.users`, `.admin`, `.analytics`, `.rs` (resource sharing).

Optional second tier — sub-area façades (`.acq.invoices`, `.acq.pols`, `.bibs.records`, `.bibs.holdings`, `.bibs.representations`) — only if the existing domain classes are big enough to justify splitting. Otherwise the methods stay flat on the domain class.

<!-- prereqs:auto:begin -->
## Prerequisites

_None — this issue has no upstream dependencies and does not have recommended soft prereqs at the toolkit level._

<!-- prereqs:auto:end -->

## Acceptance criteria

- New accessors documented in README and CLAUDE.md.
- Existing `Acquisitions(client)` / `BibliographicRecords(client)` / etc. instantiation API remains intact and supported.
- Façades are cached per client instance — repeated `.acq` access returns the same object.
- Tests covering both styles confirm they return the same data.
- Public API contract test (`tests/test_public_api_contract.py`) updated to assert the new accessors exist.
title: API: add hierarchical accessors (client.acq.invoices.get_invoice(...))

body:
**Complexity:** S
**Benefit:** Medium

## Problem

The client violates the project's own logging mandate (CLAUDE.md: "Use almaapitk.alma_logging framework — never print statements").

Concrete locations in `src/almaapitk/client/AlmaAPIClient.py`:

- L95: `print(f"✓ Configured for {self.environment} environment")`
- L366, 368, 371: prints in `test_connection`
- L386, 391: prints in `switch_environment`
- L404-442: `safe_request()` swallows every exception, prints to stdout, and returns `None`. This is actively harmful — callers can't tell `None` (no data) from `None` (network error).

## Proposal

1. Replace every `print(...)` in the client with the appropriate logger call:
   - Configuration / environment messages → `self.logger.info(...)`.
   - Connection failures in `test_connection` → `self.logger.error(...)`.
2. For `safe_request()`: either delete it outright, or convert to a typed Result-style return. Recommendation: delete it and emit a `DeprecationWarning` from a thin shim that proxies to `_request`, then drop the shim in the next major version.
3. Add a unit test that captures stdout and asserts nothing is printed during normal operation of the client.

<!-- prereqs:auto:begin -->
## Prerequisites

_None — this issue has no upstream dependencies and does not have recommended soft prereqs at the toolkit level._

<!-- prereqs:auto:end -->

## Acceptance criteria

- `grep -rn 'print(' src/almaapitk/client/` returns nothing (or only inside `if __name__ == "__main__"` example blocks).
- `safe_request` is either removed or documented as deprecated and emits `DeprecationWarning`.
- New test in `tests/` captures stdout and fails if anything is printed during a normal `get/post/put/delete` cycle.
- CHANGELOG entry calling out the behavior change for any caller currently relying on `safe_request()`.
title: Quality: replace print() with logger; remove or harden safe_request()

body:
**Complexity:** S
**Benefit:** Low

## Problem

Once `AlmaAPIClient` holds a persistent `requests.Session` (separate issue), the underlying TCP pool needs explicit teardown to release file descriptors and close keep-alive connections. There is currently no `__enter__/__exit__` or `close()` method.

## Proposal

```python
def __enter__(self):
    return self

def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc, tb):
    self.close()

def close(self):
    session = getattr(self, "_session", None)
    if session is not None:
        session.close()
        self._session = None
```

Usage:

```python
with AlmaAPIClient("SANDBOX") as alma:
    invoice = alma.get("almaws/v1/acq/invoices/123").json()
# session closed cleanly here
```

<!-- prereqs:auto:begin -->
## Prerequisites

**Hard blockers — must merge before this issue is ready to start:**
- #3 — Persistent requests.Session

<!-- prereqs:auto:end -->

## Acceptance criteria

- `with AlmaAPIClient(...) as alma:` works.
- `alma.close()` is callable directly and is idempotent (calling it twice does not raise).
- Calling any HTTP method after `close()` raises a clear error (or transparently re-creates the session — pick one and document).
- Test exercising the with-statement path.
title: API: add context-manager support to AlmaAPIClient (with-statement)

body:
**Complexity:** L (>3 days)
**Benefit:** High

## Problem

Domain methods return `Dict[str, Any]` (e.g., `get_invoice` `domains/acquisition.py:1080`, `get_pol` :1534, `get_user` `domains/users.py:97`, `get_record` `domains/bibs.py:50`, `extract_items_from_pol_data` :1555). Callers stringly-index nested fields with no IDE help, no validation, and no self-documentation. This is the largest user-facing ergonomic gap in the package.

```python
# Today
inv = acq.get_invoice("123")
vendor_code = inv["data"]["vendor"]["value"]   # typo-prone, untyped

# After
inv = acq.get_invoice("123", parse=True)
vendor_code = inv.vendor.code                  # IDE autocompletion + validation
```

## Proposal

- New module `src/almaapitk/models/` with Pydantic v2 models for the high-traffic objects: `Invoice`, `InvoiceLine`, `PoLine`, `Item`, `Vendor`, `Fund`, `User`, `Bib`, `Holding`, `Representation`. Each modeled against representative sandbox payloads.
- Gate behind extras: `pip install almaapitk[models]`. Library imports cleanly without Pydantic installed; attempting to use `parse=True` without it raises a clear ImportError.
- Add typed convenience either via a `parse=True` kwarg on existing methods OR parallel `*_typed` methods. Decide and document — recommend `parse=True` to keep the surface area small.
- Models opt into `extra='allow'` so Alma adding a new field doesn't break parsing.

<!-- prereqs:auto:begin -->
## Prerequisites

**Recommended (would simplify or improve this work, but not strictly blocking):**
- #20 — OpenAPI-driven request/response validation (OpenAPI specs become the schema source for generated models)

<!-- prereqs:auto:end -->

## Acceptance criteria

- Migration path documented (CHANGELOG + docs/).
- Phase 1 ships at least `Invoice`, `PoLine`, `User` modeled and tested against fixtures captured from sandbox.
- Models are validated against real responses via a fixture-based test suite.
- Public API export added in `almaapitk.__init__` lazy-import map.
- Decision recorded: `parse=True` kwarg vs. `*_typed` parallel methods.

## Notes

This is the largest item in the improvement pool. Can ship per-domain — Acquisitions first (highest LOC and most complex callers), then Users, then Bibs. Treat each domain as its own milestone.
title: API: add optional Pydantic response models for hot-path payloads

body:
**Complexity:** M
**Benefit:** Medium

## Problem

Domain methods that walk paged Alma endpoints either expose `limit`/`offset` to the caller (e.g., `list_invoices(limit, offset)` in `src/almaapitk/domains/acquisition.py:1394`, `search_invoices` at :1445, `get_invoice_lines` at :1487, `search_records` in `domains/bibs.py:75`, `process_users_batch` in `domains/users.py:481`) or hand-roll the loop. There is no shared paginator, so each domain re-derives the offset bookkeeping and total_record_count handling.

## Proposal

A generator method on `AlmaAPIClient`:

```python
def iter_paged(self, endpoint, *, params=None, page_size=100,
               record_key=None, max_records=None):
    """Yield records one at a time, fetching pages on demand.

    record_key: e.g. 'invoice', 'pol', 'user' — the key under which the array lives
                in the Alma response payload.
    max_records: hard cap; None means yield until exhaustion.
    """
    offset = 0
    yielded = 0
    while True:
        page_params = {**(params or {}), "limit": page_size, "offset": offset}
        body = self.get(endpoint, params=page_params).json()
        items = body.get(record_key, []) if record_key else []
        for item in items:
            if max_records is not None and yielded >= max_records:
                return
            yield item
            yielded += 1
        total = body.get("total_record_count", 0)
        offset += page_size
        if offset >= total or not items:
            return
```

Existing domain methods become thin wrappers — `list_invoices` calls `self.client.iter_paged("almaws/v1/acq/invoices", record_key="invoice", ...)` and either yields or `list(...)`s the result.

<!-- prereqs:auto:begin -->
## Prerequisites

**Recommended (would simplify or improve this work, but not strictly blocking):**
- #4 — Consolidate HTTP verbs into _request() (cleaner via the single _request() chokepoint)

<!-- prereqs:auto:end -->

## Acceptance criteria

- New `client.iter_paged(...)` method with the signature above (or equivalent).
- At least two existing domain methods migrated as proof points; their tests still pass.
- Documented in CLAUDE.md as the preferred way to walk results.
- `_fetch_all_pages()` references in CLAUDE.md / python-dev-expert skill aligned.
- Generator semantics (yield, not list) preserved — callers that need a list use `list(...)`.
title: API: add iter_paged() generator at the client level

body:
**Complexity:** S
**Benefit:** Medium

## Problem

Alma error responses include `errorList.error[0].trackingId`, which Ex Libris support uses to investigate cases. Domain logging code already references it (e.g., `tracking_id=getattr(e, 'tracking_id', None)` in users.py and the patterns documented in CLAUDE.md), but `_handle_response` (`src/almaapitk/client/AlmaAPIClient.py:118-152`) never extracts it, so the attribute is always `None`. This is a silent dead-code path — logs look like they capture tracking IDs but actually never do.

## Proposal

In `_handle_response`, after extracting `error_msg`, also pull `trackingId` and `errorCode` and attach them to the exception:

```python
tracking_id = None
alma_code = ""
try:
    err = error_data["errorList"]["error"]
    err = err[0] if isinstance(err, list) else err
    tracking_id = err.get("trackingId")
    alma_code = str(err.get("errorCode") or "")
except (KeyError, TypeError, IndexError):
    pass

exc_cls = self._classify_error(response.status_code, alma_code)
exc = exc_cls(error_msg, response.status_code, response)
exc.tracking_id = tracking_id
exc.alma_code = alma_code
raise exc
```

Update `AlmaAPIError.__init__` to default these to `None`/`""` so the attributes always exist.

<!-- prereqs:auto:begin -->
## Prerequisites

**Hard blockers — must merge before this issue is ready to start:**
- #9 — Map Alma error codes to specific exception subclasses

<!-- prereqs:auto:end -->

## Acceptance criteria

- `AlmaAPIError` exposes `tracking_id` and `alma_code` attributes (both default-initialized).
- `_handle_response` populates them from the response payload when present.
- Logger output formats include them (or domain code that already references them now actually receives values).
- Test fixture asserting both attributes round-trip from a representative error payload.
title: Errors: propagate Alma tracking_id and error_code onto raised exceptions

body:
**Complexity:** M
**Benefit:** High

## Problem

`_handle_response` raises a single `AlmaAPIError` for everything (`src/almaapitk/client/AlmaAPIClient.py:118-152`). Domain code that wants to handle "duplicate invoice" (Alma error 402459) or "POL not in the right mode" (40166411) currently has to inspect the error message string, which is brittle and translates poorly across Alma releases. The `alma-api-expert` skill already enumerates these codes — they just aren't typed.

## Proposal

A registry mapping Alma error code → `AlmaAPIError` subclass, plus HTTP-status fallbacks.

```python
class AlmaAuthenticationError(AlmaAPIError): ...    # HTTP 401
class AlmaRateLimitError(AlmaAPIError): ...         # HTTP 429
class AlmaServerError(AlmaAPIError): ...            # HTTP 5xx
class AlmaResourceNotFoundError(AlmaAPIError): ...  # HTTP 404
class AlmaDuplicateInvoiceError(AlmaAPIError): ...  # code 402459
class AlmaInvalidPolModeError(AlmaAPIError): ...    # code 40166411
# AlmaValidationError already exists; keep it.

ERROR_CODE_REGISTRY = {
    "402459":   AlmaDuplicateInvoiceError,
    "40166411": AlmaInvalidPolModeError,
    # extend as we encounter more
}

def _classify_error(self, status_code, alma_code):
    if alma_code in ERROR_CODE_REGISTRY:
        return ERROR_CODE_REGISTRY[alma_code]
    if status_code == 401:                  return AlmaAuthenticationError
    if status_code == 404:                  return AlmaResourceNotFoundError
    if status_code == 429:                  return AlmaRateLimitError
    if 500 <= status_code < 600:            return AlmaServerError
    return AlmaAPIError
```

Caller code changes from:

```python
except AlmaAPIError as e:
    if "duplicate" in str(e).lower():
        ...
```

to:

```python
except AlmaDuplicateInvoiceError:
    ...
```

<!-- prereqs:auto:begin -->
## Prerequisites

_None — this issue has no upstream dependencies and does not have recommended soft prereqs at the toolkit level._

<!-- prereqs:auto:end -->

## Acceptance criteria

- All new exception classes inherit from `AlmaAPIError` so existing `except AlmaAPIError:` blocks keep working.
- Public API exports the new classes from `almaapitk.__init__` and the lazy-import map in `_lazy_imports`.
- `tests/test_public_api_contract.py` extended with the new symbols.
- Registry lives at module level so it can be extended with one-line additions as we discover more codes.
- Codes are documented in a single table, cross-linked with the `alma-api-expert` skill.

## Related

Tracking-id propagation (separate issue) — should land together so the new exceptions actually carry useful diagnostic info.
title: Errors: map Alma error codes to specific exception subclasses

body:
**Complexity:** M
**Benefit:** Medium

## Problem

There is no client-side throttle. Bulk operations rely on Alma to push back via 429 — with no retry adapter, those become hard failures; even with one, every 429 burns a retry slot. A polite client throttles itself first.

## Proposal

A rolling 60-second window of request timestamps maintained on the client; pause if the window is full. Pattern is documented in CLAUDE.md "Archived Advanced Features Documentation" / archived `BaseAPIClient`.

```python
def _enforce_rate_limit(self):
    if self._rate_limit is None:
        return
    now = time.time()
    self._request_times = [t for t in self._request_times if now - t < 60]
    if len(self._request_times) >= self._rate_limit:
        sleep_for = 60 - (now - self._request_times[0])
        if sleep_for > 0:
            self.logger.warning("Rate limit reached; sleeping",
                                rate_limit_per_min=self._rate_limit,
                                sleep_seconds=sleep_for)
            time.sleep(sleep_for)
    self._request_times.append(time.time())
```

Called once at the top of `_request(...)` (after the verb-consolidation issue lands).

<!-- prereqs:auto:begin -->
## Prerequisites

**Hard blockers — must merge before this issue is ready to start:**
- #3 — Persistent requests.Session
- #4 — Consolidate HTTP verbs into _request()

<!-- prereqs:auto:end -->

## Acceptance criteria

- Constructor kwarg `rate_limit: int | None = 100` — None disables.
- Throttle event is logged at WARNING with sleep duration.
- Documented as RPM (requests per minute) — 100 RPM is conservative; Alma's actual cap is ~25 RPS / institution.
- Disabled-by-default vs. enabled-by-default decision recorded in CHANGELOG. Recommendation: enabled at 100 RPM by default.
title: HTTP: implement client-side rolling-window rate limiting

## Problem

`self.base_url = "https://api-eu.hosted.exlibrisgroup.com"` is hardcoded at `src/almaapitk/client/AlmaAPIClient.py:93`. Anyone outside the EU forks the package or monkey-patches the attribute. Trivial to add now, painful to add later once external callers depend on it being a constant.

## Proposal

```python
REGION_HOSTS = {
    "EU":   "https://api-eu.hosted.exlibrisgroup.com",
    "NA":   "https://api-na.hosted.exlibrisgroup.com",
    "APAC": "https://api-ap.hosted.exlibrisgroup.com",
    "CA":   "https://api-ca.hosted.exlibrisgroup.com",
    "CN":   "https://api-cn.hosted.exlibrisgroup.com",
}

def __init__(self, environment="SANDBOX", region="EU", host=None):
    ...
    if host is not None:
        self.base_url = host
    else:
        region = region.upper()
        if region not in REGION_HOSTS:
            raise ValueError(f"Unknown region {region!r}; valid: {sorted(REGION_HOSTS)}")
        self.base_url = REGION_HOSTS[region]
```

body:
**Complexity:** S
**Benefit:** High

## Problem

Any 429/502/503/504 from the Alma API surfaces immediately as `AlmaAPIError`. Long-running scripts (user-expiry runs, bulk POL receiving, paged invoice listing) fail mid-flight and have to be re-run from scratch. CLAUDE.md "Archived Advanced Features Documentation" already documents this as the intended design — it just hasn't been re-implemented.

## Proposal

Mount a `urllib3.util.Retry` adapter on the session.

```python
from requests.adapters import HTTPAdapter
from urllib3.util.retry import Retry

retry = Retry(
    total=3,
    status_forcelist=[429, 500, 502, 503, 504],
    backoff_factor=1,                     # 1s, 2s, 4s
    allowed_methods=frozenset({"GET", "POST", "PUT", "DELETE"}),
    respect_retry_after_header=True,
)
adapter = HTTPAdapter(max_retries=retry)
self._session.mount("https://", adapter)
self._session.mount("http://", adapter)
```

Expose knobs via constructor kwargs with sensible defaults: `max_retries=3`, `backoff_factor=1`. Advanced callers may pass a fully-built `Retry` object.

<!-- prereqs:auto:begin -->
## Prerequisites

**Hard blockers — must merge before this issue is ready to start:**
- #3 — Persistent requests.Session

<!-- prereqs:auto:end -->

## Acceptance criteria

- Retries are observable via the existing logger at WARNING level (one log entry per retry, including target URL, status, and attempt number).
- `respect_retry_after_header=True` so we honor Alma's `Retry-After`.
- Constructor exposes `max_retries`, `backoff_factor` (or a `retry: Retry` instance escape hatch).
- POST/PUT retries are explicitly opt-in or scoped to truly idempotent operations — review carefully; some POSTs (invoice creation) are NOT idempotent.
title: HTTP: add retry with exponential backoff for 429 / 5xx

body:
**Complexity:** M (1–3 days)
**Benefit:** High

## Problem

`get`, `post`, `put`, and `delete` in `src/almaapitk/client/AlmaAPIClient.py:157-353` each repeat ~25 lines of: build URL, prepare headers, log_request, time the call, invoke `requests.<verb>`, log_response, optionally debug-log the body, then `_handle_response`. The duplication makes any cross-cutting change (timeout, retry, rate-limit, body redaction, header injection) cost four edits and inevitable drift.

## Proposal

A single internal entry point:

```python
def _request(self, method, endpoint, *, params=None, data=None,
             content_type=None, custom_headers=None, timeout=None) -> AlmaResponse:
    url = self._build_url(endpoint)
    headers = self._prepare_headers(content_type)
    if custom_headers:
        headers.update(custom_headers)
    self.logger.log_request(method, endpoint, params=params, headers=headers, body=data)

    request_kwargs = {"headers": headers, "params": params,
                      "timeout": timeout if timeout is not None else self.timeout}
    if isinstance(data, dict) and not content_type:
        request_kwargs["json"] = data
    elif data is not None:
        request_kwargs["data"] = data

    start = time.time()
    response = self._session.request(method, url, **request_kwargs)
    duration_ms = (time.time() - start) * 1000

    self.logger.log_response(response, duration_ms=duration_ms)
    body = _safe_response_body(response)
    if body:
        self.logger.debug(f"{method} response body from {endpoint}",
                          endpoint=endpoint, status_code=response.status_code,
                          response_data=body)
    return self._handle_response(response)
```

Public verbs become two-liners: `def get(self, endpoint, **kw): return self._request("GET", endpoint, **kw)` etc. Public signatures preserved.

<!-- prereqs:auto:begin -->
## Prerequisites

**Hard blockers — must merge before this issue is ready to start:**
- #3 — Persistent requests.Session

<!-- prereqs:auto:end -->

## Acceptance criteria

- Existing public signatures of `get/post/put/delete` are preserved bit-for-bit.
- `_request` is the only place that calls `self._session.request`.
- A `_safe_response_body(response)` helper replaces the four `try: response.json() except: None` blocks.
- All existing tests pass.
title: HTTP: consolidate get/post/put/delete into a single _request() method

body:
**Complexity:** S (≤½ day)
**Benefit:** High

## Problem

Every call to `AlmaAPIClient.get/post/put/delete` issues a fresh `requests.<method>(...)` call (`src/almaapitk/client/AlmaAPIClient.py:180,238,241,299,302,347`). That means a new TCP+TLS handshake per request. For multi-call workflows (bulk user updates, POL receiving, paged invoice listing) this is the dominant per-call cost.

It also blocks two downstream improvements: retry adapters and rate limiting, both of which want to hang off a persistent session.

## Proposal

Hold a single `requests.Session` for the lifetime of the client.

```python
class AlmaAPIClient:
    def __init__(self, environment="SANDBOX"):
        ...
        self._session = requests.Session()
        self._session.headers.update(self.default_headers)
```

All HTTP methods route through `self._session.request(method, url, ...)`. Per-call `custom_headers` still wins via the per-call `headers=` kwarg.

<!-- prereqs:auto:begin -->
## Prerequisites

_None — this issue has no upstream dependencies and does not have recommended soft prereqs at the toolkit level._

<!-- prereqs:auto:end -->

## Acceptance criteria

- A single `requests.Session` is created in `__init__` and reused across all four verbs.
- Default headers move from per-call dicts onto `session.headers`; per-call overrides still work.
- `tests/test_public_api_contract.py` and `scripts/smoke_import.py` still pass.
- Existing domain methods need no changes.

## Notes

Prerequisite for: retry adapter, client-side rate limiting, context-manager support (separate issues).
title: HTTP: use a persistent requests.Session for connection pooling

body:
## Symptom

Every log line emitted via `almaapitk.alma_logging` includes a stray `(taskName=None)` field, even when the calling code is synchronous and never set anything called "taskName". Example from a real consumer running on Python 3.12.3:

```
[2026-04-29 12:50:22] INFO     [api_client] Retrieved collection members for collection 81343516030004146 (taskName=None)
[2026-04-29 12:50:22] DEBUG    [api_client] Retrieved 20 MMS IDs in this batch (taskName=None)
[2026-04-29 12:50:22] INFO     [api_client] Retrieved 220 total MMS IDs from collection (taskName=None)
```

This is harmless functionally (the field is always `None`) but adds noise to every log line and confuses operators who pass a `--task` argument to their script and then see `taskName=None` everywhere — looks like a bug in their config when it isn't.

## Root cause

Python 3.12 added a new built-in attribute to `logging.LogRecord` called `taskName`, which holds the name of the `asyncio.Task` the log record was emitted within (or `None` for sync code). See [Python 3.12 release notes](https://docs.python.org/3/whatsnew/3.12.html#logging).

`src/almaapitk/alma_logging/formatters.py` has a `standard_attrs` set used to filter out built-in LogRecord attrs from the "custom context fields" tail. The set was written before Python 3.12 and does not include `'taskName'`:

```python
# src/almaapitk/alma_logging/formatters.py
standard_attrs = {
    'name', 'msg', 'args', 'created', 'filename', 'funcName', 'levelname',
    'levelno', 'lineno', 'module', 'msecs', 'message', 'pathname', 'process',
    'processName', 'relativeCreated', 'thread', 'threadName', 'exc_info',
    'exc_text', 'stack_info', 'getMessage', 'domain', 'environment'
}
```

Result: when running on Python 3.12+, every `LogRecord` carries a `taskName` attribute, the formatter sees it as a "custom" field, and emits it in the output.

## Fix

Add `'taskName'` to the `standard_attrs` set. (Probably worth checking the [LogRecord attributes table](https://docs.python.org/3/library/logging.html#logrecord-attributes) for any other recently-added standard attributes while you're in there, in case the same drift will recur.)

## Affected versions

Anyone using `almaapitk` 0.3.1 (and prior PyPI-published versions) on Python 3.12+. Since `pyproject.toml` requires `python = ">=3.12"`, this affects every supported install.

## Suggested fix shape

One-line change in `formatters.py`. Probably ships as `0.3.2`. Could be bundled with other small fixes from the cherry-pick backlog (see `feature/subscription-continuous-orders` `BRANCH_STATUS.md` for the fund-distribution methods).

## Reproducibility

```python
from almaapitk import AlmaAPIClient
client = AlmaAPIClient("SANDBOX")
client.test_connection()
# Every emitted log line will have `(taskName=None)` appended
```
title: Logging: stray '(taskName=None)' on every log line under Python 3.12+

body:
Follow-up to the manual first publish (`almaapitk 0.3.1`, 2026-04-27).

Now that the project exists on PyPI, configure PyPI Trusted Publisher (OIDC) and add a `.github/workflows/release.yml` that builds and publishes on tag push. This eliminates manual `twine` commands and removes API tokens from `~/.pypirc` entirely.

## Scope (for the follow-up spec)

- Configure Trusted Publisher on https://pypi.org/manage/account/publishing/ for repo `hagaybar/AlmaAPITK`
- Add `.github/workflows/release.yml`: triggered on tag push matching `v*.*.*`, runs `poetry build`, then publishes via `pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish`
- Optional: TestPyPI publish on every push to `main` for continuous validation
- Internal consumer migration plan (per repo, lazily) — separate sub-task

## Lessons from the manual first release worth carrying over

- Both `pyproject.toml version` and `src/almaapitk/__init__.py __version__` must be bumped together; CI should fail if they diverge.
- Don't use `exclude = ["**/*"]` in pyproject.toml — Poetry applies it after `packages` and produces an empty wheel. Use the explicit per-directory denylist already in `pyproject.toml`.
- Test/Real PyPI both prohibit re-uploading the same version even after deletion. The pre-release verification gate must be able to bump the version if a defect is found before real PyPI publish.
- Smoke scripts at `scripts/post_publish/` should run as part of the release workflow, in a fresh venv against the just-published artifacts.
- Audit gate (ruff/bandit/vulture/grep + manual review) should be enforced in CI as a non-blocking job, with findings surfaced for triage.

## References

- Design spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-27-pypi-publishing-design.md` (§13 names this as Phase 5 / out of scope of the manual publish)
- Manual recipe: `docs/releases/HOW_TO_RELEASE.md`
- Audit findings: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-27-pypi-publishing-audit-findings.md`
title: Approach 3: Trusted Publisher (OIDC) + GitHub Actions release workflow
